sglang.title: fix: zero_init buffer
sglang.author: yyihuang
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Flashinfer v0.2.11.post3 feature: enable PDL, trtllm-gen attention zero_init buffer
Fix crash at running gptoss introduced by this flashinfer update:
flashinfer-ai/flashinfer#1463
How to re-produce the crash:
python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model openai/gpt-oss-120b --tp 8 --port 40000

sglang.title: [2/n]decouple quantization implementation from vLLM dependency
sglang.author: AniZpZ
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The primary goal of this change is to enhance the consistency and stability of SGLang's quantization features. By decoupling the quantization implementation from its vLLM dependency, we aim to make the module easier to maintain and more portable.

sglang.title: [sgl-kernel] 1/N Refactor sglang cutlass 3x - gemm fp8 blockwise sm90
sglang.author: yuan-luo
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
This PR is to refactor sglang cutlass 3x - gemm fp8 blockwise sm90. The sweet point is to make the cutlass lib wrapper in sglang well-structured and be easy to extend for various GPU archs.
The most code base is adapted from vllm, with some sglang customized change as vllm cutlass version

sglang.title: [sgl-kernel] Support FlashInfer top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits
sglang.author: yuan-luo
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
This PR is to introduce FlashInfer top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits kernel into SGLang, which is expected to improve long context inference performance. E2E benchmark result shows slightly improvement in max_concurrency==1.
The principle of the op is to adjust the order of softmax and red

sglang.author: yitianlian
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

This PR introduces a model_version tracking capability for SGLang server deployments. With this feature, each deployed model is associated with a specific version that can be queried or updated.
It is essential to identify which version of a model is currently deployed, particularly in r

sglang.title: [VLM] Improving multimodal tensor hash kernel
sglang.author: adarshxs
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Improving hash kernel security+performance wise
#8054
#8342
current hash kernel uses .sum() - bad reduction and no avalanche properties.
Modifications
The new hash function. we do a per-word mixing using Murmur3 fmix32.
Final 64-bit SplitMix64 runs on CPU.
Benchmark & Profiling
Resulting

vllm.title: [Quantization]: Support compressed-tensors mixed-precision model loading
vllm.author: dsikka
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Check for the presence of a per-config_group defined format
If present, use it to select the correct scheme
If not present, use global format
This will allow loading of models with multiple compressors (e.g NVPF4 with FP8) while maintaining backwards compatibility

Testing:
We can now load

vllm.title: [Core] Return final response for aborted requests from `AsyncLLM.generate`
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Currently no more outputs are returned for aborted requests, which makes sense for the API server when aborting due to closed connections. However there are other use cases where AsyncLLM requests are aborted out-of-band and it may be useful for the generate() caller to receive a final output with t

vllm.title: [BugFix] Fix initial DP request load imbalance
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The internal DP load-balancing logic currently only sends requests to the first rank until it receives the first stats update from the coordinator. This means that an initial burst of requests after startup can result in a temporary imbalance.
It was causing the load-balancing test to sometimes fail
